name: sir
description: Security runtime for AI coding agents (Sandbox in Reverse). Protects against confused deputy attacks, credential exfiltration, and supply chain posture tampering via Claude Code hooks.

llm_interaction.data_flow_to_llm: none — sir is a local policy decision point. Tool calls are evaluated on the developer's machine by a pure-Rust policy oracle (mister-core). sir never forwards tool arguments, tool results, session state, or ledger contents to any LLM or remote service.
llm_interaction.data_flow_from_llm: sir receives tool invocations via Claude Code hook payloads on stdin, parses the intent (verb, target, metadata), and returns an allow/ask/deny verdict. sir does not call back into the model.
prompt_injection_defenses: MCP response scanning for authority-framing, exfiltration, credential harvesting patterns
Credential output scanning (18 regex patterns, Luhn, SSA area validation, Shannon entropy)
Path-based IFC labeling at tool boundaries

telemetry_opt_in.destination: user-controlled SIEM collector (Splunk HEC, CrowdStrike LogScale, Datadog, Elastic, etc.)
telemetry_opt_in.secret_redaction: secret-labeled targets SHA-256 hashed; network targets reduced to hostname only via RedactTarget
telemetry_opt_in.reason_field_privacy: block/deny reasons redacted; only verb + target + verdict emitted

policy_oracle.purpose: Pure function: lease + session + intent = allow/ask/deny verdict

classifier.technique: prefix-based lexical classification with compound command splitting, shell wrapper unwrapping, and regex pattern matching
honest_limitations: Shell command classification is prefix-based. Interpreter one-liners (python -c, node -e) are detected; scripts that exfiltrate internally (python myscript.py) are not visible.
IFC labels track tool boundaries, not model-internal reasoning. Paraphrased secrets are not caught.
sir cannot distinguish developer-initiated vs injection-initiated tool calls.
Semantic injection analysis is out of scope. Pattern matching only.

claim: sir contains zero AI/ML components. It is a deterministic, local policy decision point that protects AI coding agents without itself embedding models, weights, training data, prompts, or any machine-learning runtime.
generated_by: hand-maintained; bump version field in lockstep with cmd/sir/version.go on each release
